starting_dimensions.notes: Per-piece sawed slug from Ø42 mm round bar. Finished envelope is Ø39.79 x 14 mm; this provides about 2.6 mm radial OD stock and about 6 mm total axial stock for saw cleanup, facing, heat-treat/lapping allowance, and handling. For 700 pieces, process as a batch from long bars with dedicated soft jaws/mandrels.

stock.why: The part is a small coaxial cylindrical external gear with a central bore, so round bar is the nearest standard form. Although 700 pieces is medium volume, the blank is simple and small; without confirmed repeat volume or forging die economics, Ø42 round bar is the lower-risk provisional choice. Dedicated mandrels/soft jaws are still justified for the batch.

evidence.visual_location: lower right title block, material row

evidence.cited_text: Envelope approximately Ø39.79 mm by 14 mm; envelope_shape cylindrical.
Shop supports alloy steel machining, heat treatment, gear manufacturing, grinding, honing, and lapping in-house.
Round bar selected provisionally over forging due missing die amortization data for 700-piece batch.

route.route_name: Round-bar soft-turn, hob/shave, case-harden, hard-finish gear route
route.route_reason: The part is a small rotational helical gear. The cost-effective route is to prepare a turned gear blank from round bar, generate the soft gear teeth, deburr before heat treatment, case-harden and temper, then hard-finish the bore, datum faces, and gear flanks to control H7 bore size, runout, surface finish, and DIN gear quality.

setups.setup_name: Saw blank preparation from round bar
setups.machine_reason: Sawing is the lowest-cost in-house method to convert Ø42 mm alloy-steel round bar into repeatable short slugs for the 700-piece batch.

workholding.grip_description: Clamp long Ø42 mm round bar in saw vise and index to slug length.

setups.stock_state_before: Ø42 mm 16MnCrS5 round bar in machinable pre-heat-treatment condition.
setups.stock_state_after: Sawn Ø42 x 17 mm cylindrical slugs, raw saw faces, ready for lathe Op10.

sub_operations.operation_name: Saw Ø42 bar into 17 mm long gear blanks
sub_operations.reason: Prepare individual blanks with axial stock for facing and post-heat-treatment finishing allowance.

tool.tool_description: Carbide or bimetal cutoff blade suitable for alloy steel bar
tool.notes: Use batch stop/indexing for repeat length.

sub_operations.notes: Saw length is provisional stock allowance, not a finished dimension.

setups.setup_reason: Blank preparation must precede all turning operations.

setups.setup_name: CNC lathe Op10: establish datum A and semi-finish bore
setups.machine_reason: The bore, datum face, and blank geometry are coaxial rotational features; CNC lathe is the correct low-cost machine for facing, axial drilling, boring, and chamfer turning.

workholding.method: 3-jaw chuck / soft jaws on raw OD
workholding.grip_description: Grip the raw Ø42 OD with enough protrusion to face the +Z side and machine the through bore.
workholding.fixture_notes: Batch soft jaws recommended for 700 pieces to improve repeatability and reduce marking.

setups.stock_state_before: Sawn Ø42 x 17 mm slug with raw saw faces.
setups.stock_state_after: Datum face A established on +Z side; central bore drilled and bored undersize for hard finishing; +Z bore entry chamfer added.

sub_operations.operation_name: Face +Z side to establish datum face A
sub_operations.reason: Datum A is referenced by the gear runout control, so the datum face is established before gear cutting and subsequent datum-based operations.

tool.tool_description: CNMG/WNMG style OD/facing insert for alloy steel

sub_operations.notes: This is the first controlled datum surface.

sub_operations.operation_name: Center drill on gear axis for Ø9 bore
sub_operations.reason: Spotting on the rotational axis prevents drill walk before drilling the central through bore.

tool.tool_description: Short rigid spot drill for axial bore start
tool.notes: Tool diameter is a shop selection; final feature diameter is Ø9.

sub_operations.operation_name: Drill pilot through for central Ø9 bore
sub_operations.reason: Remove central material before boring. Hole depth is below the deep-hole threshold for the final Ø9 bore, so standard drilling is sufficient.

tool.tool_description: Carbide or HSS cobalt drill for alloy steel
tool.notes: Pilot diameter leaves boring stock; CAM may adjust exact drill size.

sub_operations.notes: Pilot diameter is a process selection to leave stock for boring and hard finishing.

sub_operations.operation_name: Rough/semi-finish bore through to Ø8.90 before heat treatment
sub_operations.reason: Creates a concentric locating bore for mandrel workholding and gear cutting while leaving stock for post-heat-treatment ID grinding/honing to Ø9 H7.

tool.tool_description: Micro boring bar for Ø9 class through bore

sub_operations.notes: Final H7 is not attempted before heat treatment.

sub_operations.operation_name: Turn +Z bore entry chamfer 0.7 at 30°
sub_operations.reason: Chamfer the datum-side bore mouth after boring and before heat treatment; final bench will remove any hard-finishing burrs.

tool.tool_description: Internal chamfer tool set to 30° included interpretation

sub_operations.notes: Exact interpretation of 0.7 / 30° is flagged as a clarification.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Verify datum face cleanup and bore runout/size sufficient for mandrel loading.
setups.setup_reason: Datum A and bore axis must be established before finishing OD and gear blank geometry.

setups.setup_name: CNC lathe Op20: finish soft gear blank OD, width, and chamfers
setups.machine_reason: The OD, opposite face, and axial chamfers are rotational features sharing the Z axis; a CNC lathe on a bore mandrel minimizes coaxial error before gear cutting.

workholding.method: expanding mandrel through semi-finished bore with datum A against stop
workholding.grip_description: Locate on the Ø8.90 semi-finished bore and seat datum face A against a positive axial stop; support as needed for batch repeatability.
workholding.fixture_notes: Dedicated expanding mandrel or precision arbor is justified for 700 pieces and improves gear blank concentricity.

setups.stock_state_before: Op10 blank with datum face A and Ø8.90 semi-finished through bore; OD still raw Ø42 and axial length oversize.
setups.stock_state_after: Soft-machined gear blank with OD/head diameter Ø39.79, width left at approximately 14.05 mm for later lapping, opposite face machined, OD chamfers and second bore chamfer complete.

sub_operations.operation_name: Face -Z side to 14.05 mm pre-lap width
sub_operations.reason: Establish the opposite axial face and leave small allowance for post-heat-treatment face lapping to final 14.0 mm width.

tool.tool_description: Facing insert suitable for alloy steel

sub_operations.notes: Final width is achieved after heat treatment by lapping.

sub_operations.operation_name: Rough turn OD from Ø42 to Ø40.50 over gear width
sub_operations.reason: Remove bulk stock from the round bar blank before the finishing OD pass and gear hobbing.

tool.tool_description: CNMG/WNMG roughing insert for alloy steel

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish turn gear blank OD/head diameter to Ø39.79 +0/-0.05
sub_operations.reason: Set the gear blank outside/head diameter before hobbing; the drawing specifies Ø39.79 +0/-0.05 and notes the head is milled during toothing.

tool.tool_description: Finishing insert for steel with small nose radius

sub_operations.notes: OD tolerance clarification remains open but route uses extracted value.

sub_operations.operation_name: Turn 2X external axial edge chamfers 0.3 x 45°
sub_operations.reason: The OD chamfers modify the intersections of the external gear/OD with both axial faces and should follow OD finishing.

tool.tool_description: 45° turning chamfer tool

sub_operations.notes: Final hand deburr after gear cutting and hard finishing will preserve specified chamfers.

sub_operations.operation_name: Turn -Z bore entry chamfer 0.7 at 30°
sub_operations.reason: Completes the second bore mouth chamfer after the opposite face is established.

tool.tool_description: Internal chamfer tool for bore mouth

setups.inspection_at_setup: Check OD/head diameter, pre-lap width, bore location on mandrel, and chamfer presence before gear cutting.
setups.setup_reason: Completes the soft rotational blank before gear generation.

setups.setup_name: In-process inspection of soft gear blank
setups.machine_reason: Dimensional inspection is needed before committing the batch to gear hobbing and heat treatment.

workholding.method: bench inspection fixtures and bore plug
workholding.grip_description: Support part on datum face and check bore/OD/width with plug gauges, micrometers, and runout fixture.
workholding.fixture_notes: Production go/no-go gauges may be justified for 700 pieces.

setups.stock_state_before: Soft-machined gear blank after lathe Op20.
setups.stock_state_after: Accepted soft gear blank released to gear hobbing.

sub_operations.operation_name: Inspect soft blank OD, width allowance, datum face, and semi-finished bore
sub_operations.reason: Prevents adding gear-cutting and heat-treatment cost to blanks with incorrect datum, bore, OD, width, or chamfer condition.

tool.tool_description: Micrometer, bore gauge/plug, height gauge, and runout fixture

setups.setup_name: Gear hobbing: generate 39-tooth LH helical gear
setups.machine_reason: External helical gear teeth are best generated on a gear hobbing machine; the shop has dedicated hobbing machines for external gear cutting.

workholding.grip_description: Locate on semi-finished bore with datum face A against a spacer or shoulder; support axially to control runout.
workholding.fixture_notes: Dedicated hob arbor/mandrel required for repeatable 700-piece gear cutting.

setups.stock_state_before: Soft turned blank Ø39.79 with Ø8.90 semi-finished bore and pre-lap width.
setups.stock_state_after: Soft hobbed left-hand helical gear tooth form with stock for shaving/grinding.

sub_operations.operation_name: Hob 39-tooth left-hand helical gear, mn=1, β=15°, α=20°
sub_operations.reason: Generate the external helical tooth form after the blank OD, bore, and datum face are established.

tool.tool_description: Module 1 DIN 3972/II hob for 20° pressure angle, left-hand helical setup
tool.notes: Tooth tip rounding must be confirmed before final tool/process release.

sub_operations.notes: Operation uses gear-hobbing machine although formula category is profile_milling/fixed gear generation.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Check first-off tooth count, helix hand, root diameter, and over-teeth gauge before full batch.
setups.setup_reason: Gear teeth must be generated before shaving and heat treatment.

setups.machine_reason: Gear shaving improves soft gear tooth accuracy and surface quality before case hardening, reducing hard finishing stock and risk.

workholding.grip_description: Mount on bore-locating gear arbor with axial datum face support.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use the same datum logic as hobbing to preserve concentricity.

setups.stock_state_before: Soft hobbed gear blank with generated teeth.
setups.stock_state_after: Soft shaved gear teeth with improved flank form, ready for deburr and heat treatment.

sub_operations.operation_name: Shave gear flanks before case hardening
sub_operations.reason: Improves tooth form and surface before heat treatment and reduces hard grinding/honing load.

tool.tool_description: Shaving cutter matched to mn=1, z39, β=15° LH gear data
tool.notes: Final cutter data depends on confirmed tooth rounding.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Inspect over-teeth gauge and visual burr condition on first-off shaved gear.
setups.setup_reason: Soft gear finishing before heat treatment helps achieve final gear quality with less hard finishing.

setups.machine_reason: Burrs from hobbing and shaving must be removed before heat treatment; hardened burrs are difficult to remove and can affect case uniformity.

workholding.grip_description: Handle gears in trays and support by faces; avoid damaging gear teeth.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use soft brushes/stones appropriate for gear teeth.

setups.stock_state_before: Soft shaved gear with possible tooth and bore-mouth burrs.
setups.stock_state_after: Deburred and cleaned soft gear ready for case hardening.

sub_operations.operation_name: Deburr gear tooth edges, bore mouths, and unspecified sharp edges before heat treatment
sub_operations.reason: Remove soft burrs before hardening to prevent hardened burrs and inspection errors.

tool.tool_description: Fine abrasive brush, ceramic stone, and deburr blades
tool.notes: Avoid rounding functional gear flanks beyond specification.

sub_operations.operation_name: Clean parts before sealed-quench heat treatment
sub_operations.reason: Remove chips, oil, and abrasive debris before carburizing/quench to avoid contamination and case defects.

tool.tool_description: Aqueous/degreasing clean suitable before heat treatment

setups.inspection_at_setup: Visual burr check before heat treatment.
setups.setup_reason: Burr management before hardening is mandatory for gear quality.

setups.machine_reason: The drawing requires case-hardened and tempered 16MnCrS5 at 630+100 HV10 with Eht 0.3+0.2 mm; the shop has sealed-quench carburizing/tempering furnaces in-house.

workholding.grip_description: Load gears in baskets or fixtures to minimize tooth damage and distortion; avoid heavy stacking on gear teeth.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use batch fixturing appropriate for small gears and distortion control.

setups.stock_state_before: Soft machined, hobbed, shaved, deburred, and cleaned gears.
setups.stock_state_after: Case-hardened and tempered gears with expected distortion allowance for hard finishing.

sub_operations.operation_name: Case harden and temper 16MnCrS5 to 630+100 HV10, Eht 0.3+0.2 mm
sub_operations.reason: Required part-level heat treatment to create hardened wear surfaces on gear teeth and part surfaces.

tool.tool_description: Gas carburizing/case-hardening and tempering furnace cycle
tool.notes: Furnace cycle parameters are determined by heat-treatment department, not by route planner.

sub_operations.notes: All heat treatment is in-house; no outside process needed.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Hardness/case-depth coupon or sample check per heat-treatment quality plan.
setups.setup_reason: Heat treatment is required before final hard finishing of critical surfaces.

setups.setup_name: Post-heat-treatment face lapping of datum faces
setups.machine_reason: Face lapping/double-disc lapping is the most suitable in-house process to restore flat, parallel hardened axial faces and finish the 14 mm width without excessive cutting forces.

workholding.grip_description: Carry gears by OD/tooth-safe carrier, lap both axial faces with controlled stock removal.
workholding.fixture_notes: Carrier must protect gear teeth and maintain parallelism.

setups.stock_state_before: Case-hardened gear with axial width allowance and possible heat-treat distortion.
setups.stock_state_after: Finished axial faces including datum A; gear width brought to final 14.0 mm.

sub_operations.operation_name: Lap datum face A and opposite face to final 14.0 mm width
sub_operations.reason: Restores datum face quality after heat treatment and finishes the critical gear width before bore and gear hard finishing reference operations.

tool.tool_description: Face lapping plates/abrasive slurry for hardened steel
tool.notes: Exact stock removal controlled by inspection.

sub_operations.notes: If width tolerance is later clarified as loose, this step may be replaced by face grinding or omitted; retained here to protect datum/runout quality after heat treat.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Check final width and face parallelism/flatness before bore honing and gear grinding.
setups.setup_reason: Datum faces must be clean and stable for final bore/gear inspection and finishing references.

setups.machine_reason: The case-hardened Ø9 bore requires a hard finishing process; ID grinding is appropriate to correct heat-treat distortion and prepare for final H7 honing.

workholding.method: locate on lapped faces and OD/tooth-safe support
workholding.grip_description: Hold gear square to lapped datum face and grind bore coaxially; protect finished gear teeth.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use small-bore ID grinding fixture for Ø9 through bore.

setups.stock_state_before: Case-hardened gear with lapped faces and undersize bore.
setups.stock_state_after: Bore ground close to final size with small honing allowance.

sub_operations.operation_name: ID grind Ø9 bore from Ø8.90 to approx. Ø8.98 through 14 mm
sub_operations.reason: Remove heat-treat distortion and leave minimal stock for bore honing to final Ø9 H7 and fine finish.

tool.tool_description: Small ID grinding wheel suitable for Ø9 hardened steel bore
tool.notes: Wheel/quill size is a process selection.

sub_operations.notes: Final size is achieved by honing, not grinding alone.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Gauge bore after grinding before honing.
setups.setup_reason: Corrects hardened bore geometry before final H7 honing.

setups.setup_name: Final bore honing to Ø9 H7
setups.machine_reason: Bore honing is the most capable in-house process for final Ø9 H7 size and fine bore texture after hardening.

workholding.grip_description: Support gear squarely and hone through the Ø9 bore with controlled expansion stone/tool.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use bore-honing mandrel appropriate for Ø9 mm bore.

setups.stock_state_before: Hardened gear with ID ground bore approximately Ø8.98.
setups.stock_state_after: Final Ø9 H7 through bore with assumed fine Rz 2.5 bore finish.

sub_operations.operation_name: Hone central through bore to Ø9 H7 over 14 mm depth
sub_operations.reason: Achieves final H7 bore size and the assumed local fine bore finish after heat treatment.

tool.tool_description: Expandable honing mandrel/stones for Ø9 hardened steel bore
tool.notes: Final expansion and abrasive grade set by honing department.

sub_operations.notes: Surface finish callout location is a clarification item.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Air gauge or bore gauge check Ø9 H7 before gear grinding.
setups.setup_reason: Final bore becomes the precise locating feature for final gear hard finishing and inspection.

setups.machine_reason: After case hardening, gear grinding is the correct in-house process to correct distortion and achieve DIN gear quality, runout, and tight gear composite/gauge requirements.

workholding.method: finished bore arbor with datum A face stop
workholding.grip_description: Mount gear on finished Ø9 H7 bore arbor and seat datum face A against a precision stop to maintain runout relationship.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use dedicated precision arbor for Ø9 H7 bore and gear grinding.

setups.stock_state_before: Case-hardened gear with lapped faces and final honed bore; teeth shaved but distorted by heat treatment.
setups.stock_state_after: Hard ground gear flanks/root geometry to final gear accuracy, ready for teeth honing.

sub_operations.operation_name: Gear grind helical tooth flanks to DIN 58405 / 8d requirements
sub_operations.reason: Correct heat-treat distortion and achieve specified gear quality, runout to datum A, gauge over teeth, and composite error limits.

tool.tool_description: Dressed gear grinding wheel/profile for mn=1, z39, β=15° LH gear
tool.notes: Grinding program depends on final confirmed tooth rounding/profile data.

sub_operations.notes: Critical operation for final gear accuracy.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Check first-off gear chart and runout after grinding.
setups.setup_reason: Hard finishing of gear teeth must occur after heat treatment and after bore/datum references are finished.

setups.machine_reason: Teeth honing is available in-house and is appropriate to improve hardened gear tooth surface finish and running behavior after grinding.

workholding.grip_description: Locate on Ø9 H7 bore and datum face A, matching the gear grinding datum strategy.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use tooth-safe finishing fixture and honing tool matched to gear geometry.

setups.stock_state_before: Hard ground gear teeth with final bore and faces.
setups.stock_state_after: Final honed gear teeth meeting local Rz 6 assumption and gear finishing requirements.

sub_operations.operation_name: Teeth hone final hardened gear flanks
sub_operations.reason: Improves final tooth surface texture and contact quality after grinding; supports the local Rz 6 tooth/external surface requirement.

tool.tool_description: Honing tool matched to z39, mn=1 helical gear
tool.notes: Abrasive grade selected by gear finishing department.

sub_operations.notes: Rz is not converted to Ra; inspection should measure the required Rz parameter.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Visual and surface finish spot check after honing.
setups.setup_reason: Final tooth finishing follows gear grinding.

setups.setup_name: Final deburr, edge break, clean, and mark
setups.machine_reason: Manual finishing is required after all machining and hard finishing so burrs do not affect final inspection; the shop has in-house finish bench capability.

workholding.grip_description: Support on soft pads or trays; avoid damaging finished teeth and honed bore.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use non-marring handling for hardened finished gears.

setups.stock_state_before: Fully hard-finished gear with possible fine grinding/honing burrs at edges.
setups.stock_state_after: Deburred, edge-broken, cleaned, and marked part ready for final inspection.

sub_operations.operation_name: Final deburr gear tooth edges, bore edges, and axial edges
sub_operations.reason: Final deburring must occur before inspection so burrs do not affect measurements or create rejectable sharp edges.

tool.tool_description: Fine abrasive brush, nylon brush, ceramic stone
tool.notes: Do not damage gear profile or enlarge bore chamfers.

sub_operations.operation_name: Verify/finish unspecified edge break per general note
sub_operations.reason: Ensures all unspecified sharp edges comply with the general deburr/edge-break condition after final hard finishing.

tool.tool_description: Fine abrasive/nylon brush and ceramic stone

sub_operations.operation_name: Final clean after deburr
sub_operations.reason: Remove abrasive debris and loose burrs before marking and final inspection.

tool.tool_description: Final wash/dry equipment
tool.notes: Ensure bore and tooth spaces are clean.

sub_operations.operation_name: Mark/identify batch per shop quality practice
sub_operations.reason: Part identification should occur after cleaning and before final inspection/packaging without affecting functional surfaces.

tool.tool_description: Batch tag, laser/ink mark, or packaging label as allowed by quality plan
tool.notes: Do not mark gear teeth or H7 bore unless explicitly allowed.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Visual burr and cleanliness check before final inspection.
setups.setup_reason: All machining and hard finishing must be complete before final bench finishing and inspection.

setups.machine_reason: The shop has in-house dimensional inspection, gear measurement, surface finish measurement, and hardness testing required for the H7 bore, gear quality, runout, surface texture, and case-hardness requirements.

workholding.grip_description: Use Ø9 bore arbor, datum face reference, gear measuring center, bore gauge/air gauge, surface tester, and hardness/case-depth checks as required.
workholding.fixture_notes: Gear inspection arbor must match final Ø9 H7 bore and datum A reference.

setups.stock_state_before: Finished, deburred, cleaned, and marked gear.
setups.stock_state_after: Accepted finished gear ready for packaging/shipment.

sub_operations.operation_name: Final inspect Ø9 H7 bore, gear quality, runout to datum A, width, chamfers, surface finish, and hardness
sub_operations.reason: Final conformance check after deburr/cleaning, including all critical dimensional, gear, datum, finish, deburr, and heat-treatment requirements.

tool.tool_description: Gear measuring center, bore gauge/air gauge, micrometers, runout fixture, surface finish tester, hardness/case-depth testing
tool.notes: Measure Rz values as Rz, not converted Ra.

sub_operations.notes: Final inspection is intentionally after all deburr, cleaning, and marking steps.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Final acceptance record / gear inspection chart.
setups.setup_reason: Final quality gate after all processing.

routing_notes: All selected machine_family values are exact keys from the supplied shop machine_inventory.
No outside processes are required; heat treatment, gear cutting, grinding, honing, lapping, finishing, and inspection are all available in-house.
Setup time fields are set to 0.0 because setup-time estimation is intentionally not calculated at this planning stage.
Round-bar stock is the provisional route for a 700-piece batch. If this becomes repeat annual production, re-evaluate a near-net forged blank using actual die cost and per-piece savings.
Final inspection is placed after deburr and cleaning, per manufacturing sequence requirements.

clarifications.question: The gear data table row for tooth tip rounding appears to show a question mark for 'Kopfrundung / tooth tip rounding'. What is the required tooth tip rounding value?
clarifications.why_it_matters: The exact tooth tip/root rounding affects hob selection, shaving/grinding tool dressing, finished tooth profile, and inspection criteria.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use DIN 3972/II basic rack/tooling unless a specific rounding value is provided.

clarifications.question: Please confirm whether the local Rz 2.5 surface texture callout applies to the Ø9 H7 bore or to the adjacent axial datum face.
clarifications.why_it_matters: It determines whether bore honing is mandatory for finish or whether datum face lapping/superfinishing must be held to the finer texture.
clarifications.suggested_default: Apply Rz 2.5 to the Ø9 H7 bore.

clarifications.question: Please confirm that the 0.7 and 30° callouts describe matching chamfers at both mouths of the Ø9 H7 bore.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The bore entry geometry affects lathe chamfer tools, final deburr limits, and inspection acceptance.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat as 2X bore entry chamfers, 0.7 mm at 30°.

clarifications.question: Please confirm the unilateral tolerance on the outside/head diameter is Ø39.79 +0/-0.05 mm.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The OD/head diameter controls the turned gear blank and gear tooth head geometry before and after tooth generation.

clarifications.question: Is the 700-piece quantity a one-time order or repeat annual production where forging tooling can be amortized?
clarifications.why_it_matters: For repeat production, an in-house near-net forged blank may be lower total cost than machining from Ø45 round bar; for a one-time batch, round bar is lower risk.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use Ø45 round bar for this provisional 700-piece route.

assumptions.text: Quantity 700 is treated as a medium-volume batch; dedicated soft jaws, mandrels, and gear arbors are justified, but a new forging die is not assumed without amortization data.
16MnCrS5 round bar is supplied in a machinable pre-heat-treatment condition suitable for turning, hobbing, shaving, and deburring before case hardening.
The local Rz 2.5 finish is assumed to apply to the Ø9 H7 bore, so the route includes post-heat-treatment ID grinding and bore honing.
The bore entry geometry is treated as 2X 0.7 mm chamfers at 30° at both axial mouths of the Ø9 bore.

Until clarified, the tooth tip/root rounding is handled using the DIN 3972/II basic rack/toothing data in the feature graph; final hob and grinding wheel dressing must be confirmed before production release.

analysis_trace.summary: The part is a small Ø39.79 x 14 mm cylindrical external left-hand helical gear with z=39, mn=1, β=15°, central Ø9 H7 through bore, datum face A on +Z, 0.035 mm runout of the gear/OD to datum A, case-hardened 16MnCrS5 material, OD chamfers, bore entry chamfers, and general deburr requirement.

The geometry is fundamentally cylindrical/rotational, but the manufacturing route is hybrid because it requires turning, gear cutting/finishing, heat treatment, grinding/honing/lapping, and inspection rather than only lathe work.
issues: No prismatic milling features are present.

analysis_trace.summary: Material, machine availability, tolerances, and machine envelopes all pass. The shop has alloy-steel machining, CNC turning, gear hobbing/shaving/grinding/honing, sealed-quench heat treatment, ID grinding, bore honing, lapping, finish bench, and inspection in-house. Feasibility is marked with risks due heat-treatment distortion and unresolved gear/surface details.
issues: Post-heat-treatment finishing is required to protect Ø9 H7 bore and gear accuracy.
Unresolved gear tooth tip rounding affects final gear-tooling details.

analysis_trace.summary: Round bar Ø45 mm x 20 mm sawed slugs are selected provisionally because the finished part is a small cylindrical gear. The route uses batch workholding and dedicated arbors for 700 pieces. Forging was considered but not selected without repeat-volume/die-amortization data.
issues: If this is repeat annual production, an in-house near-net forged blank may become more economical.

analysis_trace.summary: Setups are sequenced as saw blanking, soft turning datum/bore/OD/chamfers, in-process inspection, gear hobbing, gear shaving, pre-HT deburr/clean, case harden/temper, post-HT face lapping, ID grinding, bore honing, gear grinding, teeth honing, final bench finishing, and final inspection.
issues: Multiple precision datum transfers require dedicated arbors and careful inspection.

analysis_trace.summary: Each setup is decomposed into atomic operations with feature IDs and geometric formula inputs. Bore and gear features are intentionally rough/semi-finished before heat treatment and finished after heat treatment.
issues: Gear-specific cycle formulas require post-processor support; geometric inputs are provided as diameter, face width/profile length, and relevant source parameters.

analysis_trace.summary: All feature IDs F001 through F008 are covered by at least one sub-operation. The process state flows logically from raw bar to sawed blank, soft blank, gear cut blank, heat-treated part, hard-finished part, bench-finished part, and final inspection. Formula inputs include non-null numeric geometry for every sub-operation.
issues: Some process allowance values such as pilot drill diameter, pre-hone bore size, and lapping allowance are planning estimates and are flagged through input_sources/assumptions.
